title: Improved Enchanting
description: Definitive Edition v2 for 1.21.11 Just Came out, Come here if you want a vanilla - friendly overhaul of the enchanting system, with new enchantments, and a new enchanting system.
body:
# **Improved Enchanting**

## Overview
This mod adds some new enchantments and makes them easier to understand by putting them into seperate exlusive categories, and makes less used enchantments more viable than they were before. Definitive Eddition V2.0 is OUT, and available for the latest version of the game (1.21.11), adding Elytra, Axe, and Crossbow Enchantment upgrades. I will work on making it available for the next update, and updates to come.

### Enchantment Exlusivity Rules
For all enchantments, there are 4 tiers of enchantments ("other" is not affected), primary, which is affecting all things within its perameters, secondary affects a large group of entities, like all undead mobs, the tertiary enchantments affect smaller groups of entites, like all raiders or bosses. Special is a passive ability that affects how you use the weapon, it is probally the most important enchantment of them all. Each enchantment tier is exlusive to itself, for example you can't have 2 primary enchantments, but you can have 1 primary enchantment and 1 secondary, you have to chose between the enchantments in each level.


# Armor Enchantments


<details>
<summary>Primary Armor Enchantments</summary>

## Primary Armor Enchantments
This level incldes the enchantment, protection with a few tweaks and a new enchantment, reinforced.


<details>
<summary>Protection</summary>

### Protection
This enchantment has gone through some tweaking, it only has half the normal protection that it would normally give, and is only found in the enchanting table. With full protection 4, you get 32% general damage protection

</details>

<details>
<summary>Reinforced</summary>

### Reinforced
This enchantment gives more armor points to your player, giving 1.5 total armor bars, and an extra 4 armour toughness points. The more pieces of armor you put on with this enchantment, the bigger the upgrade is. The downside to this enchantment is that it only gives protection against direct attacks, and it is only found in ancient cities.

</details>

## Secondary Armor Enchantments
Here you will find more generalised enchantments, with bigger protection rates, it is better to mix both of these two enchantments for best results.

<details>
<summary>Melee Proteciton</summary>

### Melee Protection
This enchantment gives proteciton to all direct Melee attacks, giving 6% damage reduction per level. If you have all your armor enchanted with full Melee Protection 4 and Protection 4, it gives 128% damage reduction, but the Protection rate is hard capped at 80%, but with half of your armor with Melee Protection 4 and Full Protection 4, it gives exactly 80% damage reduction to Melee attacks. You can find this enchantment in general loot, the enchanting table and villagers.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Projectile Protection</summary>

### Projectile Protection
This enchantment gives proteciton to all direct Projectile attacks, giving 6% damage reduction per level. If you have all your armor enchanted with full Projectile Protection 4 and protection 4, it gives 128% damage reduction, but the Protection rate is hard capped at 80%, but with half of your armor with Projectile Protection 4 and Full Protection 4, it gives exactly 80% damage reduction to Projectile attacks. You can find this enchantment in general loot, the enchanting table and villagers.

</details>

## Tertiary Armor Enchantments
This level of enchantments has very specific damage types that it takes care of, using blast and Fire protection to block the Incoming damage.



<details>
<summary>Fire Protection</summary>

### Fire Protection
This enchantment gives proteciton to all Fire based attacks, giving 6% damage reduction per level. If you have all your armor enchanted with full Fire Protection 4 and protection 4, it gives 128% damage reduction, but the Protection rate is hard capped at 80%, but with half of your armor with Fire Protection 4 and Full Protection 4, it gives exactly 80% damage reduction to Fire based attacks. This enchantment also shortens the time you stay on fire for. You can find this enchantment in general loot, the enchanting table and villagers.


</details>


<details>
<summary>Blast Protection</summary>

### Blast Protection
This enchantment gives proteciton to all explosion damage, giving 6% damage reduction per level. If you have all your armor enchanted with full Blast Protection 4 and protection 4, it gives 128% damage reduction, but the Protection rate is hard capped at 80%, but with half of your armor with Blast Protection 4 and Full Protection 4, it gives exactly 80% damage reduction to Explosions. This enchantment also reduces the knockback of explosives. You can find this enchantment in general loot, the enchanting table and villagers.


</details>

## Special Enchantments
This has an assortment of some special use case enchantments, i gave the chestplate two enchantments to pick from and the pants another one to pick from, so it would have the same amount of enchantments as the helmet and the boots.


<details>
<summary>Absorbing Hearts (chestplate only)</summary>

### Absorbing Hearts
This enchantment has a chance to give you absorption on hit, blocking some damage for 5 seconds. The chance for the absorption is 10% at the start, and each level above that gives 5% extra chance for you to get absorption, and at the max level of 3, you get absorption 2, rather than 1. You can only find this enchantment in The treasure loot in the shipwrecks.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Retaliate (chestplate only)</summary>

### Retaliate
This enchantment gives you a chance to get some strength on hit, dealing much more damage in the next period of time, each level increases the time you have strength by .5 seconds after the first second. Each level also increases the damage by 3 points. The chance of the chestplate giving you strength is increased from 10% by 5% every level, up to the max level of 3, where it is 20%. This enchantment is only found in the ancient city.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Dash (leggings only)</summary>

### Dash
This enchantment is still verry buggy, and is quite confusing, when you jump up or down one block, you get a small speed boost that is maintained by jumping, (Turn off FOV effects please) This speed can scale quickly if you know what you are doing. The speed is determined by the level. This enchantment is found in regular vaults in trial chambers. Removed as of definitive version 2.

</details>



</details>


# Bow Enchantments

<details>
<summary>Bow Enchantments</summary>

## Primary Bow Enchantments
As of the beta Definitive update v1, only power exists in this enchantment level, I might change this later, but only power is in this level.

<details>
<summary>Power</summary>

### Power
This enchantment is not changed from the game, it goes up to level 5, doing much more damage. This enchantment is found in random loot, villager trades and the enchanting table.

</details>


## Secondary Bow Enchantments
This level includes some vanilla enchantments, punch is here, and peircing is now available on the bow.

<details>
<summary>Punch</summary>

### Punch
This enchantment is already in the game, and has not been altered, it hits enemies further back when hit with an arrow and goes up to two levels. This enchantment is found in random loot, villager trades and the enchanting table.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Piercing</summary>

### Piercing
This enchantment has been taken from the crossbow, and has been altered to fit the bow, it goes through mobs until it hits too many or hits the floor. At level 4 it can go through 5 mobs. This enchantment is found in random loot, villager trades and the enchanting table.

</details>

## Tertiary Enchantments
Here are some new enchantments, Firestorm and Volley, alongside flame.


<details>
<summary>Volley</summary>

### Volley
This enchantment lets you shoot up to 5 arrows, in a short line, at the cost of durability. it makes it easy to deal with mass mobs in smlaller areas, on level one it lets you shoot 3 arrows, and at level 2 it lets you shoot 5. as of the definitive update v1.1, it is found in pillager outposts

</details>

<details>
<summary>Firestorm</summary>

This is a chaotic Enchantment, causing a fireball to blow up in the area that does not damage the area around it. It does some serious damage to the mobs around it. This enchantment only has one level, doing fire spreading around 5 blocks around the mob. This enchantment is only found in the form of enchanting books found commonly in the nether fortress bridges.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Flame</summary>

This enchantment is unchanged from the base game, and goes up to two levels, lasting longer the higher level you have. This enchantment is found in the enchantment tables, random loot and villager trades.

</details>

## Special Bow Enchantments
This tier of enchantments is one of the new ones, it adds 3 enchantments, balooner, poison arrow and frozen arrow.


<details>
<summary>Balooner</summary>

### Balooner
this enchantment sends the shot enemy flying with levetaion 12 on hit. This enchantment has 3 levels, going from 0.5 seconds of levetation to 1 second, giving the mobs a great view, and a deal of damage. This enchantment is found on random loot, in the enchanting table and with villager trades.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Poison Arrow</summary>

### Poison Arrow
This enchantment gives the mob you hit poison for 5 seconds, it goes up to level 2, giving poison 2-3 (depending on level) On hit. This enchantment is found in the jungle temple chests.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Frozen Arrow</summary>

### Frozen Arrow
This enchantment freezes the mob you hit with the bow, allowing for you to easily hit it agian. There are two levels to this enchantment and with each level, you get one second of freezing, getting 2 seconds at the max level. This enchantment is found in the chest in the basement of the igloo.

</details>

</details>


# Sword Enchantments

<details>
<summary>Sword Enchantments</summary>

## Sword Enchanting Overview
There are some enchantments here to do with melee weapons, all are directly compatible on the swords, and are compatible to axes through books, except heavy hitter, swift strike and reach, which are directly compatible on axes.

## Primary Sword Enchantments
This is where you find the main two enchants of the pack, Swift Strike and Heavy Hitter. 

<details>
<summary>Swift Strike</summary>

### Swift Strike 
increases your attack speed by 1 per level, but decreases your damage by 0.5 per level, and having a max level of 6, means that you can get a weapon up to 2.2 attack speed at the cost of -3 damage. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Heavy Hitter</summary>

### Heavy Hitter
Increases your attack damage by 1.15 at level 1 and an extra .75 damage every level above it, but at the cost of lower attack speed, losing 1 attack speed per level. This enchantment can go up to the level of 6, meaning that at its max, it will give you 4.9 extra damage at the cost of having 6 slower attack speed. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades.

</details>


## Secondary Sword Enchantments
Here you will find some general damage type enchantments, all of the slayer types will be here. There are 2 new enchantments here, ender and nether slayer, alongside smite.

<details>
<summary>Undead Slayer/Smite</summary>

### Undead Slayer/Smite
This enchantment is already in the game and remains unedited as of now, other than the fact that it is exlusive to the other secondary weapons, it does 2.5 extra damage per level to undead mobs, and goes up to level 5, meaning it does 12.5 extra damage to undead mobs at level 5. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Nether Slayer</summary>
  
### Nether Slayer
Nether slayer does more damage to mobs from the nether or if you are in the nether, it does 3 extra damage per level and goes up to level 5, doing 15 damage to mobs in or from the nether at its max echanting level. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades. It is also found in ruined portals and nether fortresses.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Ender Slayer</summary>

### Ender Slayer
Ender slayer is just like nether slayer, doing more damage to mobs from the end  more damage if you are in the end, it does 3 extra damage per level up to 5, doing a maximum damage of 15 if you are in the end, or attacking mobs from the end. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades. It is also found in stronghold libraries.

</details>


## Tertiary Sword Enchantments
Here you will find more specialised damage enchantments, doing damage to a few groups of mobs, also known as the "banes".

<details>
<summary>Illager's Bane</summary>

### Illager's Bane
This does an extra 2.5 damage per level to illagers and raiders, at its max enchantment level, 5, it does 12.5 extra damage to raiders. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades, it is also found in pillager outposts.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Boss' Bane</summary>

### Boss' Bane
Dealing double the damage of the rest of the tertiary enchantments, at 5 damage per level, Boss' Bane can do some serious work if you fight a lot of bosses, doing 25 extra damage at max level to bosses. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Bane of Arthropods</summary>

### Bane Of Arthropods
Although I tried to make this enchantment more useful than it is in the base game, it is such a neiche enchantment, doing more damage to bugs, at 2.5 per level and a max of 12.5 extra damage, it is still quite useless. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Cubism</summary>

### Cubism (Yes, I know it doesn't have bane in it)
This enchantmnet came from the inspiring of hypixel skyblock, it does more damage to creepers, slimes, magma cubes and ghasts, doing 2.5 damage per level, at a max of 12.5 damage. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades.

</details>


## Special Sword Enchantments
Here, you will find many different types of enchants, from damage to health to freezing an enemy in place, this is the most interesting enchantment type out of them all.

<details>
<summary>Lifesteal</summary>

### Lifesteal
Lifesteal is a healing enchantment reviving 2 hearts by a chance when you hit a mob. This enchantment starts at a 10% chance and increases by 5% every level, up to 25% at level 4. This enchantment is found in the chests of the ancient cities.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Wither Coat</summary>

### Wither Coat
This enchant gives the wither effect on hit, damaging the enemy, it does more damage than fire aspect, lasting from 5-7.5 seconds depending what level. This enchantment only goes up to level 2, and it lasts for 7.5 seconds at this level. This enchantment is found in mineshafts and nether fortesses as of the Definitive update v1.1.


</details>

<details>
<summary>Freeze</summary>

### Freeze
This enchant gives the hit entity slowness 10 effect, completely freezing them for 2-3 seconds on hit, and this enchantment only has one level. This enchantment is only found in the lower level of the igloos.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Fire Aspect</summary>

### Fire Aspect
This enchantment is unedited, and is left the same as it was. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Poison Tipped</summary>

### Poison Tipped
This enchant is quite confusing, as it does damage to some mobs and not others, as they are poison resistant. It is quite balanced, as no matter what, it can't kill the mobs, it brings them down to half a heart using poison, and then you can make the final strike, works well with swift strike. This enchantment is only found in the chests of the jungle temples.

</details>


## "Other" Sword Enchantments
This is just a misclanious part where there are two enchants, the other exlusivity rules do not apply to these two, and they are exlusive to different things, it says in their description.

<details>
<summary>Anti Knockback</summary>

### Anti Knockback
This enchantment is quite unreliable from my experience, but it hits the mobs back less than it does normally, and goes up to 2 levels. Its only exlusive enchantment is knockback. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Reach</summary>

### Reach
This enchant allows you to reach an extra 2 blocks at its max level of 3, but it has the downside of the fact that it attacks slower by 2 attack speed, and is exlusive to sweeping edge. This enchantment is found in the enchanting table, and in various loot including villager trades.

</details>

</details>

# Axe Enchantments (Definitive version 2+)


<details>
<summary>Axe Enchantments</summary>

There are 4 axe enchantments (as of definitive v2), each having their own upsides and downsides, 
<details>
<summary>Critical Strike</summary>
Does more damage on critical hits, dealing more damage per level, going up to level 4, dealing an extra 2 damage, and 1.5 damage above the first level upon critting. It is incompatible with Shockwave, and charge, and its found when enchanting, and on random loot.


</details>

<details>
<summary>Rampage</summary>
Gives strength, and speed on hit, with increasing length according to the level, letting you easily kill many mobs, it is incompatible with critical strike. It is found in the Enchanting, and on random loot.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Charge</summary>
Does increasing damage that scales with your speed, going up to level 5, dealing 2.25x normal damage upon sprinting, and on the floor. This can easily kill many mobs, it is incompatible with shockwave, rampage, and Critical strike. Charge is found in enchanting, and random loot.

</details>
<details>
  
<summary>Shockwave</summary>
Unleashes upon critical strike, a deadly shockwave forms, dealing high amounts of damage, scaling with the level, up to level 3, at the cost of reduced attack rate, and destroying items on the floor. Shockwave is incompatible with Critical strike, Charge, and Heavy hitter. Works best with swift strike and rampage, and is found as a rare drop in trial vaults and ominous trial vaults. (one funny thing with this enchantment is that if the mob doesn't die when hit, other mobs will just kill them)


</details>

</details>

# Elytra Enchantments (definitive version 2+)

<details>
<summary>Elytra Enchantments</summary>
Provides many options for your new set of wings, giving small boosts to parts of flying.

<details>
<summary>Absorbing Wings</summary>
Gives absorption upon flying, protecting you from taking damage, taking 2-4 hearts of damage (depending on level) upon impact away from you, it is found in end cities, and is incompatible with reinforced wings.


</details>

<details>
<summary>Graceful Landing</summary>
This is a simple enchantment with one level, it prevents you from taking any fall damage when wearing the elytra, but at the cost of slower gliding speeds, it is also found in end city loot.


</details>

<details>
<summary>Protective Wings</summary>
Gives you one armor point per level, going up to level 5, replacing the armor that you would normally have with a chestplate, it is found in ancient cities, it is incompatible with absorbing wings.


</details>

</details>



## Other Changes
I have made some other changes to some of the enchantments, in order to make them more useable, or better.

<details>
<summary>Frost Walker</summary>
Frost Walker goes up to level 3, meaning you can sprint-Jump on water (yay)

</details>


<details>
<summary>Looting</summary>
Looting now works on Axes aswell as swords for easier acess to goods.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Quick Charge</summary>
Quick Charge goes up to level 5, allowing for some quick reloads, and making the Crossbow a more viable weapon in many situations.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Piercing</summary>
Piercing now works on normal bows.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Infinity</summary>
Infinity works on Crossbows, making them more feasable as a quick shooting bow with quick charge that does less damage than a bow, as it doesn't have power.

</details>


I really recomend Overflowing Bars by Fuzs for this mod/datapack

If you want to use anything from this modpack, you are fine to do so, but I would ask that you would put a link to this page.

if you have suggestions, or positive feedback on any of my mods/datapacks, you can contact me at hemiolf211@gmail.com
